# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for SQL injection vulnerabilities and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure query construction such as string concatenation, interpolation, or unsafe formatting, including second-order dynamic SQL risks.
4. Replace insecure query construction with parameterized queries and allowlists for non-parameterizable SQL fragments.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  SORTABLE_COLUMNS = %w[name price created_at updated_at].freeze
  SORT_DIRECTIONS = %w[asc desc].freeze
  VALID_CATEGORIES = %w[electronics clothing home garden sports toys books].freeze

  def search
    @products = Product.all

    search_params = params[:search]

    if search_params.present?
      if search_params[:name].present?
        @products = @products.where("name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:name])}%")
      end

      if search_params[:category].present?
        category = search_params[:category]
        if VALID_CATEGORIES.include?(category)
          @products = @products.where(category: category)
        end
      end

      if search_params[:categories].present? && search_params[:categories].is_a?(Array)
        valid_categories = search_params[:categories].select { |c| VALID_CATEGORIES.include?(c) }
        if valid_categories.any?
          @products = @products.where(category: valid_categories)
        end
      end

      if search_params[:min_price].present?
        min_price = BigDecimal(search_params[:min_price].to_s) rescue nil
        if min_price && min_price >= 0
          @products = @products.where("price >= ?", min_price)
        end
      end

      if search_params[:max_price].present?
        max_price = BigDecimal(search_params[:max_price].to_s) rescue nil
        if max_price && max_price >= 0
          @products = @products.where("price <= ?", max_price)
        end
      end

      if search_params[:in_stock].present? && search_params[:in_stock] == "true"
        @products = @products.where("stock_quantity > ?", 0)
      end

      if search_params[:brand].present?
        @products = @products.where(brand: search_params[:brand])
      end

      if search_params[:rating_min].present?
        rating_min = search_params[:rating_min].to_f
        if rating_min.between?(0, 5)
          @products = @products.where("average_rating >= ?", rating_min)
        end
      end

      if search_params[:created_after].present?
        date = Date.parse(search_params[:created_after]) rescue nil
        if date
          @products = @products.where("created_at >= ?", date.beginning_of_day)
        end
      end

      if search_params[:created_before].present?
        date = Date.parse(search_params[:created_before]) rescue nil
        if date
          @products = @products.where("created_at <= ?", date.end_of_day)
        end
      end

      sort_column = SORTABLE_COLUMNS.include?(search_params[:sort_by]) ? search_params[:sort_by] : "created_at"
      sort_direction = SORT_DIRECTIONS.include?(search_params[:sort_direction]) ? search_params[:sort_direction] : "desc"
      @products = @products.order(Arel.sql("#{sort_column} #{sort_direction}"))
    end

    page = (search_params&.dig(:page) || 1).to_i
    per_page = (search_params&.dig(:per_page) || 25).to_i
    per_page = [[per_page, 1].max, 100].min

    @products = @products.limit(per_page).offset((page - 1) * per_page)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  private

  def sanitize_sql_like(string, escape_character = "\\")
    pattern = Regexp.union(escape_character, "%", "_")
    string.gsub(pattern) { |x| [escape_character, x].join }
  end
end
